summary:
Wormian bones indicate normal cranial bone structure variation without suggesting abusive trauma; microfractures, corner fractures, and bucket-handle fractures strongly suggest non-accidental injury patterns commonly associated with domestic violence. \boxed{A}
The presence of wormian bones signifies natural anatomical features devoid of any indication for abuse, whereas classic markers like microfractures, corner fractures, and bucket-handle fractures point towards potential instances of inflicted harm consistent with domestic violence scenarios. Therefore, A stands out as an exception among these choices regarding their relevance to diagnosing domestic violence. \boxed{A}
